attacking_style: Long-range aggressor who launches race-shaping moves from distance (48km at Tour, bridging near final climbs). Attacks primarily to soften opposition or test commitments rather than solo to victory.
team_tactics: Used as expendable aggressor to isolate rivals through repeated softening attacks before team leader's decisive move. Often operates without direct domestique support, launching isolated bridging or breakaway efforts.
terrain_preference: Suited to technical local roads and medium-mountain stages where bridging efforts and positioning matter. Strong enough to chase at 3km but not to sustain long solo efforts against organized pursuit.
vulnerability: Frequently isolated without team support depth in critical moments. Team hierarchy confusion creates tactical ambiguity — attacks sometimes contradict stated GC priorities, exposing internal leadership conflicts.
key_patterns: Launches ultra-long attacks (48km) designed to trigger winning moves for others, not himself. (2 instances)
Repeatedly isolated without domestique support when attacking or chasing, revealing team depth issues. (3 instances)
Team role confusion: attacks conflict with supporting designated GC leaders, suggests hierarchy problems. (3 instances)
Deployed as softening tool before teammate's decisive move, especially in Grand Tours. (2 instances)
Visible frustration with team orders indicates personal ambition clashing with domestique duties. (2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 shows consistent tactical deployment for UAE but without personal results. Strong enough to shape races but not finish them, suggesting role acceptance over progression.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 3
